title: butyrospermum parkii subsp. niloticum
intro: Butyrospermum parkii subsp. niloticum, also known as the African shea tree, is a subspecies of the shea tree, a plant native to Africa, valued for its nutrient-rich seeds and oil. The medicinal properties of butyrospermum parkii subsp. niloticum include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of using shea butter from this plant include moisturizing and protecting the skin, reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as providing antioxidant protection against cell damage. Herbal preparations of butyrospermum parkii subsp. niloticum include shea butter, which is often used topically in skincare products, and shea oil, which is used in cosmetics and pharmaceutical applications.
